title: sida hemitropousa
intro: Sida hemitropousa, a plant species belonging to the Malvaceae family, is commonly known as the "hemitropous mallow" or " Indian mallow". Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Sida hemitropousa include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antispasmodic and diuretic properties. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made by infusing its leaves and flowers in water or by making a decoction, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
